key_insight: A nonlocal variational phase-field framework models dynamic fracture without explicit crack tracking by coupling nonlocal elasticity with an integral crack-surface functional and irreversible phase evolution.
engineering_relevance: The approach can simulate crack initiation, branching, shear fracture, and fragmentation in elastic solids while recovering classical local behavior as nonlocal interactions shrink.
method: Uses nonlocal kinematics and kernel-dependent function spaces to relax regularity requirements.
Introduces a nonlocal crack-surface functional as the integral analogue of Ambrosio-Tortorelli regularization.
Solves the coupled momentum balance and phase-field evolution with a scalar auxiliary-variable scheme or a staggered alternating scheme, both with finite elements.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports successful numerical tests on Mode-I fracture, dynamic branching, Kalthoff-Winkler-type shear fracture, and fragmentation, with crack-tip speeds below 0.6cR and an inclined shear crack path of about 48°.
watch_item: The model’s behavior and predicted fracture scales depend on the chosen nonlocal interaction kernel/domain, so calibration and computational cost may be important in practice.
paper_title: A Variational Nonlocal Phase-Field Model for Dynamic Fracture in Elastic Solids
